diagrams.title: Point Processing Transformation Functions
diagrams.description: Shows various transformation curves: negative (inverted line), log (curved upward), power-law γ<1 (curved upward), power-law γ>1 (curved downward), contrast stretching (S-shaped)
elements: Log: curve rising steeply then leveling
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how different transformations map input to output intensity values

diagrams.title: Bit-Plane Decomposition
diagrams.description: Shows 8 binary bit planes for a sample 8-bit grayscale image, with MSB at top and LSB at bottom
diagrams.purpose: Understand how individual bits contribute to final image appearance and compression potential

diagrams.title: Histogram Equalization Process
diagrams.description: Shows input histogram, CDF curve, and output histogram after equalization
elements: Mapping function from input to output
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how equalization redistributes gray levels for contrast enhancement

diagrams.title: Contrast Stretching Transformations
diagrams.description: Shows different types of contrast stretching: linear, threshold, and smooth piecewise
elements: Input-output plot with different break points
Linear stretch: straight line with slope >1
diagrams.purpose: Understand how different piecewise linear functions stretch or compress intensity ranges
